title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

Why You'll Love This Garlic Butter Steak

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly seared New York Strip Steak with melted garlic butter on a white plate
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a juicy, tender New York Strip Steak, bathed in rich garlic herb butter for an unforgettable dinner.

description: Experience the ultimate steak dinner with this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe. Perfectly seared to a golden-brown crust and basted in aromatic garlic herb butter, each bite is incredibly juicy and flavorful. This impressive yet simple dish brings restaurant-quality steak right to your home kitchen.

ingredients.notes: freshly ground, plus more for seasoning

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove steaks from the refrigerator 30-60 minutes before cooking to bring them to room temperature. Pat them completely dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Preheat Skillet
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed cast iron skillet or other oven-safe skillet over high heat until it just begins to smoke, about 5-7 minutes. Add the olive oil to the hot skillet.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side for a medium-rare steak, creating a beautiful deep brown crust. Adjust cooking time based on desired doneness.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add the cold butter cubes, smashed garlic cloves, and fresh rosemary and thyme sprigs to the skillet. Tilt the pan slightly and, using a spoon, continuously baste the melted garlic butter over the steaks for 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking (Optional)
instructions.text: If your steaks are very thick or you prefer a more well-done steak, you can transfer the skillet to a preheated oven at 375F (190C) for an additional 3-5 minutes, or until desired internal temperature is reached. Continue basting if preferred.

instructions.title: Rest the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the steaks from the skillet and place them on a cutting board or plate. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for at least 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and juicy steak.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: Slice the rested steaks against the grain into thick pieces. Drizzle with any remaining garlic butter from the pan or fresh melted butter. Serve immediately with your favorite sides.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always pat your steaks very dry before searing; moisture on the surface will steam the meat instead of creating a crust.
For the best flavor, use high-quality, fresh ingredients for your garlic butter, especially fresh garlic and herbs.
Resting the steak is crucial. Skipping this step will result in juices running out, leaving your steak dry and less flavorful.
article:
New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter is more than just a meal; it's a celebration of classic American culinary excellence. This dish takes a prime cut of beef and elevates it with the simple yet profound flavors of rich butter and aromatic garlic, creating an unforgettable dining experience. Whether you’re planning a special occasion dinner or simply want to treat yourself to a restaurant-quality meal at home, mastering the art of cooking a New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter is a skill worth acquiring. It's a foundational American dinner recipe that promises robust flavor and a tender, juicy bite with every forkful. In this comprehensive guide, we'll walk you through everything you need to know to prepare the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, from understanding the cut to achieving that coveted golden-brown sear.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip Steak, also known as the Kansas City Strip, Ambassador Steak, or Club Steak, is a highly prized cut of beef that comes from the short loin of the cow. This area is known for producing tender and flavorful steaks because the muscles there are not heavily worked. Characterized by its fine texture, a good balance of marbling (intramuscular fat), and a firm, chewy bite, the New York Strip offers a robust beefy flavor that many steak aficionados prefer over more tender cuts like the filet mignon. It typically has a fat cap on one side, which, when rendered properly, adds immense flavor and moisture to the steak. Its rectangular shape and consistent thickness make it an ideal candidate for pan-searing, grilling, or broiling, ensuring even cooking and a beautiful crust.

## Why You'll Love This Garlic Butter Steak

There are countless reasons why the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter combination is a beloved classic. First and foremost is the unparalleled flavor profile. The inherent richness of the New York Strip is beautifully complemented by the savory, pungent notes of fresh garlic infused into melted butter. This creates a deeply satisfying umami experience that coats every bite. Beyond flavor, you'll love the incredible aroma that fills your kitchen as the garlic butter sizzles and bastes the steak – it’s an instant mood enhancer.

Furthermore, this dish offers a perfect balance of tenderness and chewiness, providing a truly gratifying texture. The process of pan-searing creates a magnificent, caramelized crust on the exterior, while the inside remains juicy and tender. It’s a dish that feels incredibly luxurious and impressive, yet it’s surprisingly straightforward to prepare, making it accessible for home cooks of all skill levels. Whether it's a weeknight indulgence or the centerpiece of a celebratory meal, this New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe delivers restaurant-quality results right in your own home, making it a staple American dinner recipe.

## Ingredients for New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

To create the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, quality ingredients are key. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **New York Strip Steaks:** Two 1.5-inch thick, bone-in or boneless steaks (about 12-16 oz each). Look for good marbling.
*   **Coarse Salt:** Such as kosher salt or sea salt, for seasoning the steak.
*   **Freshly Ground Black Pepper:** For seasoning.
*   **High Smoke Point Oil:** 1-2 tablespoons of avocado oil, grapeseed oil, or canola oil, for searing.
*   **Unsalted Butter:** 4 tablespoons (1/2 stick), cut into pats. Unsalted allows you to control the saltiness.
*   **Fresh Garlic:** 4-6 cloves, minced or thinly sliced. Adjust to your garlic preference.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** A few sprigs of fresh rosemary and/or thyme. These infuse the butter with wonderful aromatics.

## How to Cook New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter (Step-by-Step)

Achieving a perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is easier than you think. Follow these steps for a sensational result:

1.  **Prepare the Steaks:** Remove the steaks from the refrigerator at least 30-60 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. This promotes more even cooking. Pat the steaks thoroughly dry with paper towels; moisture on the surface prevents a good sear. Season generously all over with coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper. Don't be shy with the seasoning!
2.  **Preheat Your Skillet:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Let it preheat for 5-7 minutes until it's smoking hot. This extreme heat is crucial for developing a beautiful crust.
3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Add the high smoke point oil to the hot skillet. Once the oil is shimmering and just starting to smoke, carefully place the seasoned steaks in the skillet. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side without moving them, allowing a deep brown crust to form. Use tongs to sear the edges for about 30 seconds each, including the fat cap if present.
4.  **Add Garlic Butter and Herbs:** Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the unsalted butter, minced garlic, and fresh herbs to the skillet. As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the aromatic garlic butter mixture. Continue basting for 2-4 minutes, flipping the steaks once or twice, until they reach your desired doneness.
5.  **Check Doneness:** For a 1.5-inch thick steak, approximate cooking times are:
    *   **Rare:** 125-130°F (approx. 6-8 minutes total cooking time)
    *   **Medium-Rare:** 130-135°F (approx. 8-10 minutes total cooking time)
    *   **Medium:** 135-140°F (approx. 10-12 minutes total cooking time)
    Use an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the steak, avoiding bone, for the most accurate reading.
6.  **Rest the Steaks:** Once the steaks reach your desired temperature, remove them from the skillet and place them on a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This critical step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and moist steak.
7.  **Slice and Serve:** Slice the New York Strip Steaks against the grain into thick pieces. Spoon any remaining pan juices and garlic butter from the skillet over the sliced steak before serving.

## Tips for the Perfect Sear

Achieving that restaurant-quality crust on your New York Strip is paramount to a truly exceptional steak. Here are some pro tips:

*   **Room Temperature is Key:** Cold steaks cook unevenly. Allowing them to come closer to room temperature ensures the center cooks at a similar rate to the exterior.
*   **Pat Dry, Pat Dry, Pat Dry:** Any moisture on the surface of the steak will steam rather than sear, preventing the formation of a beautiful crust. Use paper towels to get them as dry as possible.
*   **Smoking Hot Skillet:** Don't be afraid of high heat. A screaming hot pan creates the Maillard reaction (browning) quickly, forming a crust before the interior overcooks.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan:** If cooking multiple steaks, work in batches if your skillet isn't large enough. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and leads to steaming.
*   **Flip Infrequently (Initially):** Let the steak sit undisturbed for a few minutes per side to develop a solid crust before flipping. Once the garlic butter is added, you can flip more often during basting.
*   **Cast Iron is Your Best Friend:** A cast iron skillet retains heat exceptionally well and distributes it evenly, making it ideal for searing steaks.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** As mentioned, resting allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in a significantly more tender and flavorful steak.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The rich and decadent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter pairs beautifully with a variety of classic American sides and beverages, creating a truly memorable meal.

For sides, consider creamy mashed potatoes, which soak up the delicious garlic butter juices, or a perfectly baked potato with all the fixings. Roasted asparagus, sautéed green beans, or creamed spinach offer a fresh, vibrant contrast to the richness of the steak. A simple green salad with a light vinaigrette can also cut through the richness and cleanse the palate.

When it comes to wine pairings, bold red wines are the classic choice. A Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, or Zinfandel will stand up to the robust flavors of the steak. For beer enthusiasts, a rich stout, porter, or amber ale can complement the savory notes. If you prefer cocktails, an Old Fashioned or a Manhattan makes a sophisticated accompaniment. Regardless of your choice, the star of the show, the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, will shine through as a truly satisfying American dinner recipe.

## Storage & Reheating

While New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is best enjoyed fresh off the skillet, you can certainly store and reheat leftovers.

**Storage:** Allow any leftover steak to cool completely before transferring it to an airtight container. Store in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. While technically possible, freezing cooked steak is not ideal, as it can significantly alter the texture upon reheating.

**Reheating:** To best preserve the texture and flavor, avoid reheating steak in the microwave if possible, as it can make the meat tough and dry.
*   **Oven Method:** Preheat your oven to 250-275°F (120-135°C). Place the steak on a wire rack set over a baking sheet. Reheat for 15-25 minutes, or until warmed through to your desired temperature. This low and slow method helps prevent overcooking.
*   **Skillet Method:** Heat a small amount of oil or butter in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add the steak and gently warm for 2-3 minutes per side, just until heated through. Be careful not to sear it again, as this can dry it out.

Even when reheated, the robust flavors of the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter will still be enjoyable, making for a delicious next-day meal.

Indulging in a perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a quintessential American dining experience that truly satisfies. From the initial sizzle in the hot pan to the final, tender bite, this recipe delivers on flavor, aroma, and satisfaction. With quality ingredients and a few key techniques, you can transform a simple cut of beef into a gourmet meal right in your own kitchen. So go ahead, treat yourself and your loved ones to this incredible garlic butter steak – it’s an American dinner recipe that promises to impress every single time.
